bibliographic_data.title: CleAR: Robust Context-Guided Generative Lighting Estimation for Mobile Augmented Reality
bibliographic_data.abstract: High-quality environment lighting is essential for creating immersive mobile augmented reality (AR) experiences. However, achieving visually coherent estimation for mobile AR is challenging due to limitations in AR device sensing capabilities, including low camera FoV and limited pixel dynamic ranges. Recent advancements in generative AI present a potential solution, but content quality and slow inference are key limitations. In this work, we design and implement a generative lighting estimation system called CleAR that can produce high-quality, diverse environment maps in the format of 360◦HDR images. Specifically, we design a two-step generation pipeline guided by AR environment context data to ensure the output aligns with the physical environment’s visual context and color appearance. To improve the estimation robustness under different lighting conditions, we design a real-time refinement component to adjust lighting estimation results on AR devices. Through a combination of quantitative and qualitative evaluations, we show that CleAR outperforms state-of-the-art lighting estimation methods on both estimation accuracy, latency, and robustness.

core_content.novelty_claim: CleAR introduces a two-step generative pipeline for generating high-quality environment maps in the format of 360◦HDR images, leveraging AR context data to ensure robust and accurate lighting estimation.
core_content.key_methodology: CleAR uses a multimodal approach combining partial LDR environment observations with generative diffusion models. The system is designed to handle real-time refinement and temporal consistency through multi-output strategies and color appearance matching.
outcomes: Achieves 51% to 56% accuracy improvement on virtual object renderings across materials and reflective properties.
Generates lighting estimates of comparable or better quality in just 3.2 seconds, over 110X faster than state-of-the-art methods.
Supports real-time refinement of lighting estimation results for robust and timely updates.
core_content.full_text_markdown: CleAR is a novel generative lighting estimation system designed to address the challenges of creating high-quality environment lighting in mobile augmented reality (AR) experiences. The system leverages AR context data, such as semantic maps and ambient light sensor data, to guide the generation of 360◦HDR environment maps from partial low dynamic range (LDR) observations. CleAR's two-step generative pipeline ensures robustness under varying lighting conditions through real-time refinement techniques. Through comprehensive evaluations, including in-lab deployment tests and user studies, CleAR outperforms existing methods in terms of accuracy, latency, and robustness.
